case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).
case.notes: User-provided full protocol with 15 min batch time, 83% isolated yield, openai council benchmark.

chemistry_plan.reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
chemistry_plan.bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
chemistry_plan.bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in nitrile oxide generation; C–H of nitroacetate during nitrile oxide formation

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are mutually compatible at room temperature; premixing in DES is acceptable since the reaction only proceeds at elevated temperature. DES is the solvent and reaction medium. Single feed simplifies pumping of viscous DES.

reagents.name: phenylacetylene
reagents.notes: Terminal alkyne; regioselective cycloaddition gives 5-phenylisoxazole regioisomer

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C; 2 equiv used to compensate for competing dimerization to furoxan

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium, no additional catalyst needed

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; may facilitate nitrile oxide generation through H-bonding activation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (EtO2C-CNO) in situ. The nitro group undergoes aci-nitro tautomerism followed by loss of water.

Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the transient ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate. The oxygen of the nitrile oxide bonds to the terminal carbon of the alkyne, placing phenyl at C-5.
Competing side reaction: dimerization of nitrile oxide to form furoxan (1,2,5-oxadiazole 2-oxide), which reduces yield and necessitates 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate.

stream_logic.reasoning: All components are compatible at room temperature; the reaction is thermally initiated at 120°C so premixing is safe. A single stream simplifies pumping of the viscous DES medium. Using one HPLC pump or syringe pump with a pre-prepared solution avoids issues with metering two viscous streams.

chemistry_plan.mixing_order_reasoning: No mixing order constraints. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are stable together at room temperature in DES. The reaction only initiates upon heating to 120°C. A single premixed feed stream is optimal given the high viscosity of the DES (~50-100 cP at RT), which would make precise T-mixer operation difficult with two separate viscous streams.
chemistry_plan.deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the reaction proceeds under air in batch.
chemistry_plan.quench_reasoning: The reaction reaches full conversion in 15 min at 120°C. In flow, cooling the outlet stream to room temperature effectively stops the reaction. No chemical quench is needed inline.
chemistry_plan.wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photoinitiation is involved.
chemistry_plan.confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide [3+2] cycloaddition). The DES viscosity is a practical concern for flow — TBAB/EG (1:5) is significantly more viscous than typical organic solvents, requiring higher pump pressures (Hagen-Poiseuille: ΔP scales linearly with viscosity). A wider-bore coil (e.g., 1.0 mm ID) or heating the feed reservoir to ~50°C to reduce viscosity may be needed. Residence time of 15 min at 120°C is well-suited for flow. Back-pressure regulator recommended to keep DES/EG below boiling point at 120°C. The 2.0 equiv of nitroacetate is needed to compensate for nitrile oxide dimerization — flow may allow reduction to ~1.5 equiv due to better temperature uniformity reducing local hot spots that promote dimerization.

final_design_candidate.human_explanation:
## Flow Setup

**Single-stream coil reactor:**
- Stainless steel tubing: 0.5 mm ID, 162 m length (31.809 mL volume)
- Feed stream A: All reagents premixed in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES at 0.067 M
- Flow rate: 3.5343 mL/min
- Residence time: 9.0 min
- Temperature: 120.0°C
- Back-pressure regulator: 5.0 bar
- HPLC piston pump required

## Why These Conditions Were Chosen

The design is based on thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition analogies showing 10× intensification factors for similar pericyclic reactions. The 9.0 min residence time represents a 10× reduction from the 90 min batch time, consistent with flow enhancement literature for thermal cycloadditions. The single premixed stream approach eliminates mixing bottlenecks that would be problematic with the high-viscosity DES (50-100 cP). The 0.5 mm ID tubing balances acceptable pressure drop (62.2 bar) with adequate mixing (r_mix = 0.116 < 0.20 threshold). Operating at 120°C matches the batch conditions required for nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate.

## Key Differences from Batch Protocol

1. **Residence time**: Reduced from 90 min (batch) to 9.0 min (flow) due to superior heat transfer
2. **Concentration**: Diluted from 0.5 M (batch) to 0.067 M (flow) to manage flow dynamics
3. **Reagent stoichiometry**: Maintained 2.0 equiv ethyl nitroacetate to compensate for nitrile oxide dimerization
4. **Reaction medium**: Continues using TBAB/EG (1:5) DES but in a flowing system
5. **Temperature control**: Isothermal operation vs. batch heating profile

## Engineering Validation Warnings

⚠️ **Critical BPR Issue**: The current 5.0 bar setting is inadequate. Safety analysis requires **62.8 bar minimum** to prevent solvent vaporization at 120°C with the calculated pressure drop.

⚠️ **Mixing Limitation**: While r_mix = 0.116 is acceptable, the high DES viscosity may cause pump cavitation. Consider pressurizing the feed reservoir to 0.5 bar above vapor pressure.

⚠️ **Unvalidated Enhancement Factor**: The 10× intensification factor is class-typical but not experimentally verified for this specific reaction. First experiments should validate conversion at design conditions.

**Confidence: LOW** — The closest literature analogy has a similarity score of 0.38 (threshold for MEDIUM: 0.50). No close precedent found in the corpus for this exact reaction class and conditions. Treat this proposal as a starting hypothesis requiring careful experimental validation before scale-up.

formatted_result.explanation:
## Flow Setup

**Single-stream coil reactor:**
- Stainless steel tubing: 0.5 mm ID, 162 m length (31.809 mL volume)
- Feed stream A: All reagents premixed in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES at 0.067 M
- Flow rate: 3.5343 mL/min
- Residence time: 9.0 min
- Temperature: 120.0°C
- Back-pressure regulator: 5.0 bar
- HPLC piston pump required

## Why These Conditions Were Chosen

The design is based on thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition analogies showing 10× intensification factors for similar pericyclic reactions. The 9.0 min residence time represents a 10× reduction from the 90 min batch time, consistent with flow enhancement literature for thermal cycloadditions. The single premixed stream approach eliminates mixing bottlenecks that would be problematic with the high-viscosity DES (50-100 cP). The 0.5 mm ID tubing balances acceptable pressure drop (62.2 bar) with adequate mixing (r_mix = 0.116 < 0.20 threshold). Operating at 120°C matches the batch conditions required for nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate.

## Key Differences from Batch Protocol

1. **Residence time**: Reduced from 90 min (batch) to 9.0 min (flow) due to superior heat transfer
2. **Concentration**: Diluted from 0.5 M (batch) to 0.067 M (flow) to manage flow dynamics
3. **Reagent stoichiometry**: Maintained 2.0 equiv ethyl nitroacetate to compensate for nitrile oxide dimerization
4. **Reaction medium**: Continues using TBAB/EG (1:5) DES but in a flowing system
5. **Temperature control**: Isothermal operation vs. batch heating profile

## Engineering Validation Warnings

⚠️ **Critical BPR Issue**: The current 5.0 bar setting is inadequate. Safety analysis requires **62.8 bar minimum** to prevent solvent vaporization at 120°C with the calculated pressure drop.

⚠️ **Mixing Limitation**: While r_mix = 0.116 is acceptable, the high DES viscosity may cause pump cavitation. Consider pressurizing the feed reservoir to 0.5 bar above vapor pressure.

⚠️ **Unvalidated Enhancement Factor**: The 10× intensification factor is class-typical but not experimentally verified for this specific reaction. First experiments should validate conversion at design conditions.

**Confidence: LOW** — The closest literature analogy has a similarity score of 0.38 (threshold for MEDIUM: 0.50). No close precedent found in the corpus for this exact reaction class and conditions. Treat this proposal as a starting hypothesis requiring careful experimental validation before scale-up.

case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).

batch_record.reaction_description: 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
batch_record.raw_text: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (EtO2C-CNO) in situ. The nitro group undergoes aci-nitro tautomerism followed by loss of water.

Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the transient ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate. The oxygen of the nitrile oxide bonds to the terminal carbon of the alkyne, placing phenyl at C-5.
Competing side reaction: dimerization of nitrile oxide to form furoxan (1,2,5-oxadiazole 2-oxide), which reduces yield and necessitates 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate.

stream_logic.reasoning: All components are compatible at room temperature; the reaction is thermally initiated at 120°C so premixing is safe. A single stream simplifies pumping of the viscous DES medium. Using one HPLC pump or syringe pump with a pre-prepared solution avoids issues with metering two viscous streams.

chemistry_plan.mixing_order_reasoning: No mixing order constraints. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are stable together at room temperature in DES. The reaction only initiates upon heating to 120°C. A single premixed feed stream is optimal given the high viscosity of the DES (~50-100 cP at RT), which would make precise T-mixer operation difficult with two separate viscous streams.
chemistry_plan.deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the reaction proceeds under air in batch.
chemistry_plan.quench_reasoning: The reaction reaches full conversion in 15 min at 120°C. In flow, cooling the outlet stream to room temperature effectively stops the reaction. No chemical quench is needed inline.
chemistry_plan.wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photoinitiation is involved.
chemistry_plan.confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide [3+2] cycloaddition). The DES viscosity is a practical concern for flow — TBAB/EG (1:5) is significantly more viscous than typical organic solvents, requiring higher pump pressures (Hagen-Poiseuille: ΔP scales linearly with viscosity). A wider-bore coil (e.g., 1.0 mm ID) or heating the feed reservoir to ~50°C to reduce viscosity may be needed. Residence time of 15 min at 120°C is well-suited for flow. Back-pressure regulator recommended to keep DES/EG below boiling point at 120°C. The 2.0 equiv of nitroacetate is needed to compensate for nitrile oxide dimerization — flow may allow reduction to ~1.5 equiv due to better temperature uniformity reducing local hot spots that promote dimerization.

analogies.summary: This photocycloaddition reaction utilizes triplet sensitization with 2,2'-dimethoxythioxanthone (2,2'-MeOTX) as the photocatalyst at 1 mol% loading under 455 nm COB LED irradiation. The flow process operates in acetonitrile at 200°C with a 5-minute residence time and 6.0 mL/min flow rate using a FlowSyn steel coil reactor (30 mL volume) equipped with a 34 bar back-pressure regulator. Flow chemistry enabled daisy-chaining of the initial photochemical step with high-temperature thermal cascade reactions, dramatically improving yield from 16% in batch (2 hours) to 88% in flow with a 24-fold intensification factor. The high-temperature operation required for the thermal cascade was much safer to implement in the continuous flow reactor compared to batch processing.

analogies.record_id: Yang 등 - 2024 - Safety Guided Process Development and Scale-Up of the Highly Energetic Compound 4-Methyl-1,2,5-oxadi.pdf
analogies.summary: This process involves the synthesis of 4-methyl-1,2,5-oxadiazole, a highly energetic compound, using a nitration or cyclization reaction in a concentrated sulfuric acid/water mixture (63:6.3 ratio). The flow process was conducted in a tubular reactor at 154°C with a residence time of 179 minutes (approximately 3 hours) and a flow rate of 0.12 mL/min, requiring a back-pressure regulator at 47.6 bar to maintain liquid phase conditions. Flow chemistry enabled a significant temperature increase from 65°C in batch to 154°C, resulting in an improved yield from 70.8% to 78.8%. The primary advantage of flow over batch was enhanced safety when handling this highly energetic compound, as the continuous process allows for better control of the exothermic reaction and reduces the inventory of hazardous material at any given time. The high pressure and temperature conditions achievable in the flow reactor also contributed to the improved reaction efficiency.

analogies.summary: This base-catalyzed nucleophilic ring-opening reaction was conducted in a cyclic continuous-flow reactor with 1.2 mm internal diameter tubing at 60°C using EtOAc as solvent. The flow process achieved 96% yield with a 60-minute residence time at 0.5 mL/min flow rate, compared to 93% yield requiring 20 hours in batch mode. The cyclic continuous-flow setup enabled a dramatic 20-fold reduction in reaction time (from 20 hours to 1 hour) while improving yield by 3 percentage points. The flow advantage likely stems from enhanced mass transfer and more consistent reaction conditions in the continuous system compared to the prolonged batch heating required for completion.

proposal.chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. Key design considerations: (1) DES viscosity requires wider bore tubing (1.0 mm ID) and higher pump pressures, (2) 120°C operation requires BPR to prevent solvent boiling, (3) 2.0 equiv nitroacetate compensates for competing nitrile oxide dimerization, (4) calculations show Pe=9 indicating significant axial dispersion but acceptable for this reaction, (5) mass transfer limited (Da=39) but single stream eliminates mixing bottleneck.
